Places

The must-see spots of the city.

playa

La Caleta Beach

Cádiz's most iconic beach, small and intimate, flanked by the castles of Santa Catalina and San Sebastián. It is the romantic heart of the c…

monumento

Cádiz Cathedral

Cádiz Cathedral, known as the New Cathedral, is a jewel of late Baroque architecture that dominates the old town with its yellow dome.

mirador

Torre Tavira

The tallest watchtower in Cádiz houses Spain's largest camera obscura. From its rooftop you can see the city's 160 historic towers.

playa

Bolonia Beach

One of the most spectacular beaches in Andalusia. Crystal-clear waters, a 30-metre dune and the Roman ruins of Baelo Claudia in the backgrou…

naturaleza

La Breña Natural Park

La Breña and Barbate Marshes Natural Park is a paradise of pines and cliffs over the Atlantic, with Caños de Meca beach at its foot.

gastronomia

Cádiz Central Market

The gastronomic heart of Cádiz. A 19th-century neoclassical market where locals buy the day's fresh fish and visitors discover local cuisine…

The light of Cádiz, in every story.
